params.text:
Information governance in care

Every day in care you handle deeply personal details: someone's health conditions, medication, care needs, family situation and finances. Information governance is simply the way we make sure this information is held, used and shared safely, lawfully and only for the right reasons.

Getting it right is not just paperwork. It protects the dignity, privacy and safety of the people you support, keeps their trust, and keeps you and your organisation on the right side of the law and the regulator.

  • The people you support trust you with their most private information.
  • Poor handling can cause real harm — distress, discrimination or even physical danger.
  • The Care Quality Commission (CQC) expects services to keep records securely and use information responsibly as part of being safe and well-led.

params.alt: Keeping information safe under UK GDPR

params.text:
The rules behind it

Three things sit behind good information governance in adult social care:

  • UK GDPR – the General Data Protection Regulation as it applies in the UK, setting out how personal data must be handled.
  • Data Protection Act 2018 – the UK law that sits alongside UK GDPR and fills in the detail.
  • The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) – the UK's independent regulator that oversees data protection and can investigate and fine organisations.

On top of these, care services follow the Caldicott Principles, which guide how confidential health and care information is used. We will meet all of these in this course.
